title: Tetractomia orbiculata
taxonomy: Tetractomia orbiculata is classified within the family Clusiaceae. This family is commonly known as the staphylocarpous family due to the unique way in which their fruit splits open. The species Tetractomia orbiculata belongs to the genus Tetractomia, which includes several other species of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The family Clusiaceae is part of the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Further classification places the order Malpighiales within the clade Rosidae, which is a major group of flowering plants.
uses: Tetractomia orbiculata is a plant species native to Africa, and it has been found to possess various medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Additionally, the plant's leaves and roots have been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and inflammation. The plant's antimalarial and antibacterial properties have also been studied, suggesting its potential use in treating certain infectious diseases. Further research is needed to fully explore the medicinal uses of Tetractomia orbiculata.
